senses.definition: to weigh something beforehand, especially to determine its weight in advance of a measurement or decision
examples: The chef preweighs the ingredients to ensure consistency.
Before packing, she preweighs the luggage to avoid excess charges.

senses.definition: to greet or receive someone in advance, or in anticipation of their arrival
examples: They prewelcome guests by sending warm emails before the event.
The team prewelcomes new members with a welcome packet.

senses.definition: had been greeted in advance, or had received a welcome beforehand
examples: The hosts prewelcomed the delegates yesterday.
Visitors were prewelcomed with a tour agenda.

senses.definition: greeting or receiving in advance; performing welcoming actions before someone arrives
examples: The committee is prewelcoming arriving researchers.
Prewelcoming sessions help reduce arrival stress.

senses.definition: having been wired or connected in advance, especially with pre-installed wiring
examples: The prewelwired panels reduced installation time.
Prewelwired homes can be set up quickly.

senses.definition: the act or process of wiring in advance
examples: Prewelwiring speeds up construction.
They focused on prewelwiring during renovation.

senses.definition: to whip in advance or beforehand, often to prepare a mixture
examples: Prewhip the cream before folding it into the dessert.
The chef prewhips the batter to achieve a smooth texture.

senses.definition: having been whipped in advance
examples: The prewhipped cream was ready for the pie.
Use the prewhipped topping for a quick finish.

senses.definition: the act of whipping something in advance
examples: Prewhipping the cream saves time later.
During prep, they noted the duration of prewhipping.

senses.definition: having a willingness prepared in advance; ready to agree or comply
examples: The staff were prewilling to assist.
He took a prewilling stance toward collaboration.

senses.definition: in a way that shows willingness in advance
examples: She prewillingly agreed to the plan.
They prewillingly joined the project early.

senses.definition: the quality of being willing in advance
examples: Her prewillingness to help impressed the organizers.
Prewillingness can speed up teamwork.

senses.definition: to wire in advance; to connect electrical or electronic systems before use
examples: They plan to prewire the office for faster installation.
Prewire the devices before shipping.

senses.definition: already wired or connected in advance
examples: A prewired home is ready for internet installation.
The car comes with prewired sensors.

senses.definition: designed to operate without wireless connections, or not relying on wireless technology
examples: The system is prewireless, using wired connections instead.
Prewireless networks may offer stable performance in interference-prone areas.

senses.definition: to wire in advance (third-person singular present) or as a general process
examples: The contractor prewires the building before insulation.
They prewires the panels for quick setup.

senses.definition: the act or process of wiring in advance
examples: Prewiring reduces on-site work.
They reviewed the prewiring plan.

senses.definition: a person who witnesses something beforehand, or a prior witness
examples: The prewitness described the scene before the main witness arrived.
Court records note the prewitness testimony.

senses.definition: to wonder about something in advance; to anticipate with curiosity
examples: They prewonder what the outcome will be.
People often prewonder about next year's trends.

senses.definition: the act or state of wondering in advance; anticipation
examples: Her prewonderment about the surprise built excitement.
There was a look of prewonderment on their faces.

senses.definition: preparatory work done before a main task or project
examples: They did prework to ensure a smooth rollout.
The prework included planning and data gathering.

senses.definition: to perform preparatory work before a main task
examples: Teams prework the specs before the meeting.
We prework the materials to save time later.

senses.definition: having completed preparatory work in advance
examples: The preworked dataset saved hours of analysis.
Preworked templates were ready for distribution.

senses.definition: the act of doing preparatory work before a main task
examples: Preworking is essential for a quick launch.
The team documented their preworking steps.

senses.definition: preparatory tasks or materials; things done before main work
examples: The preworks included data gathering and risk assessment.
They reviewed the preworks before the meeting.

senses.definition: the quality of existing or appearing to exist before the current world or worldly concerns; antiquated perspective
examples: The novel explores preworldliness and its impact on decisions.
Her preworldliness made her skeptical of trends.
